primary_legislation.england_wales: Ionising Radiations Regulations 2017 (IRR17); UKHSA radon guidance; HSE radon in workplaces guidance
primary_legislation.scotland: Ionising Radiations Regulations 2017; Public Health Scotland radon mapping; HSE guidance
primary_legislation.northern_ireland: Ionising Radiations Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2017; NIEA/UKHSA radon mapping guidance

applicable_condition: Required only for practices in radon-affected areas. Check the UKHSA (England/Wales), Public Health Scotland, or PHA NI radon potential maps for the premises postcode.
notes: Steps are identical to school MOD-023. Responsible person updated to Practice Manager. Note: GP practices in ground-floor or basement premises in high radon potential areas are at particular risk given the long daily occupancy hours of clinical staff.

logbooks.title: Radon Measurement and Action Review
logbooks.applicable_condition: Practices in areas with >1% probability of exceeding the Action Level per UKHSA / PHS / PHA NI radon maps.
logbooks.frequency_detail: Initial measurement if not previously done. Repeat every 2–5 years, following remediation works, or following significant building changes.

steps.description: Check the radon potential map for the practice postcode: UKHSA (www.ukradon.org) for England/Wales, Public Health Scotland (publichealthscotland.scot/radon) for Scotland, PHA NI for Northern Ireland.
If in an affected area, commission radon measurements from a UKAS-accredited radon measurement service.
Place CR-39 passive detectors in ground-floor and basement rooms for a minimum 3-month measurement period.
steps.photo_guidance: Photograph the detector placement location with the room reference visible.

steps.description: Return detectors and obtain the measurement report.
If any room exceeds 300 Bq/m³ (Action Level): notify UKHSA/relevant public health body, arrange radon remediation (positive pressurisation, sub-slab depressurisation), and retest after remediation.

If levels are below 300 Bq/m³ but above 100 Bq/m³ (Target Level), consider remediation as best practice. GP practice staff spend many hours in the building daily — long-term exposure risk is elevated compared to the general population.
Record: date, rooms tested, values (Bq/m³), comparison to Action Level, action taken, next measurement due date.
